# Desert Garden Guide
> Practical wisdom for cacti, native plants, and water-wise gardens in arid climates — without fighting the land.

Desert Garden Guide covers desert and arid-climate gardening for the Sonoran, Mojave, and Chihuahuan deserts. Content is practical and actionable — written for real desert conditions, not generic gardening guides designed for temperate climates with abundant rainfall.

## Topics Covered
- Desert gardening basics: soil preparation, seasonal timing, raised beds, tools, composting, containers
- Water-wise techniques: drip irrigation, deep-and-infrequent watering, rainwater harvesting, seasonal schedules
- Native plants: cacti, agave, palo verde, mesquite, ocotillo, desert willow, ironwood
- Desert wildflowers: pollinators, seasonal color, nurse plant relationships, companion planting
- Vegetable gardening: two-season calendar (cool season Oct–Mar, warm season Mar–May)

## Key Articles
- /blog/how-to-grow-vegetables-in-the-desert-a-seasonal-guide/

- /blog/creating-your-desert-garden-guide/
- /blog/the-best-time-of-year-to-plant-in-the-desert-and-why-it-matters/

## Site Structure
- /blog/ — All articles with category filter
- /about/ — About the site
- /feed.xml — RSS feed (updated with every new post)
- /sitemap.xml — Full URL sitemap

## Coverage Area
American Southwest: Phoenix AZ, Tucson AZ, Las Vegas NV, El Paso TX, Las Cruces NM, Palm Springs CA, Santa Fe NM, Flagstaff AZ, and surrounding arid regions (USDA zones 9–11).

## About
Desert Garden Guide publishes practical desert gardening guides for arid-climate gardeners. New content published regularly. No paywall — all content is freely accessible.
